repo_prior_art.cite: none; the closest catalogue entry is stat_dose_response_minimax, a fixed-dose minimax converse rather than an illness-death intervention EIF.

published_prior_art.cite: MartinussenKnudsenRytgaard2026Debiased, Theorem 4.1 gives the covariate-adjusted EIF for its natural-initiation-window intervention; the proposal's exogenous planned-initiation hazard is distinct but closely adjacent.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference / “bounded-count-diameter” studies a rare-event causal ratio obstruction, not a censored illness-death marked-point-process limit.

published_prior_art.cite: SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous establishes effective-size rates for exponentially tilted static continuous exposures; AiHuangZhang2026UniformContinuousTreatment establishes ordinary Gaussian/Bahadur continuous-treatment inference. Neither gives the proposed multistate critical marked-Poisson experiment.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_pn_weak_event_honest_inference / “bounded-count-diameter” is a close effective-count honest-diameter analogue for PN/PS ratios, but has a different estimand and no local initiation intervention.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020HonestCI supplies bias-aware local-regression CI geometry, not learned CAR illness-death scores or a bounded-effective-count calibration.

soundness_flags.label: def:exogenous-intervention and thm:exogenous-eif
soundness_flags.one_line: Because q_b vanishes after u_star+b, Qbar_b(t)=0 there and a_b=q_b/Qbar_b is 0/0; define the intervention as a hazard only on {Qbar_b>0} together with its post-window absorbing convention, and restate the product-integral accordingly.

soundness_flags.label: ass:local-positivity, def:eif-components, and thm:exogenous-eif
soundness_flags.one_line: A marginal lower bound on f_U,P does not identify alpha_12,P(u,x) or make the W_1 weight valid for covariate strata that the exogenous rule treats but the natural initiation hazard never reaches; require conditional initiation positivity/integrability over the intervention-relevant covariate support.

soundness_flags.label: def:one-step, def:critical-statistic, and oeq:critical-phase
soundness_flags.one_line: Z_n is not determined by the proposed compound-Poisson map: the displayed empirical variance makes it self-normalized and can be zero or asymptotically negligible on the no-local-initiation event, whereas Gamma_lambda uses deterministic sigma_lambda; additionally, the unspecified hazard fits give no defined remainder regime. State an oracle-score limit first, then specify a self-normalized/fallback map and sufficient nuisance control for its plug-in extension.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:observed-hazard-validity and lem:extension-invariance
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The causal equality between the full-filtration intervention and the observed transition hazards is assumed, not identified from P.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The exact-time causal interpretation and extension-invariance claim are conditional on every compatible full-data extension satisfying ass:consistency, ass:observed-hazard-validity, and ass:car.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 5–7, definition of the smoothed window intervention and Theorem 4.1
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper defines its window using the natural initiation-hazard-derived density g(u)=alpha_01^o(u) exp{-A_01^o(u)} normalized inside the window, and Theorem 4.1 gives the covariate-adjusted observed-data EIF by conditionalizing Theorem 3.1.

source_verification_receipts.locator: p. 9, Theorem 2; p. 13, Theorem 4; pp. 25–26, effective-sample-size discussion
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives a Le Cam lower bound of order sqrt(delta/n) and Gaussian inference under sqrt(n/delta) growth for exponentially tilted static continuous-exposure interventions, describing effective sample size n/delta.
The paper studies nonparametric continuous-treatment GDRF estimation by weighted local linear regression and develops uniform confidence bands via a bootstrap procedure.
The article develops honest bias-aware CIs for kernel/local-polynomial nonparametric regression, including efficiency and bandwidth/length comparisons.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of Business & Economic Statistics 44(1) (2026), pp. 67–79
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives kernel-localized, cross-fitted doubly robust inference for continuous-treatment average dose-response functions and partial effects under nuisance-rate conditions.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(546) (2024), pp. 1541–1553; bibliographic record and abstract
source_verification_receipts.locator: Abstract/record; full journal theorem text was not accessible in this review
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The cited paper exists and concerns machine-learning inference for treatment-specific survival curves; no formula-level comparison was used in this review.
The article studies minimax CATE estimation in a Hölder-smooth nonparametric model and constructs a local-polynomial estimator under high-level minimax-optimality conditions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Separate the oracle critical experiment from the estimated-score extension: give the exact self-normalized marked-Poisson functional, define the no-local-initiation fallback, and only then state concrete nuisance-rate conditions under which cross-fitting has the same limit.

Replace marginal f_U positivity with a conditional intervention-overlap condition sufficient to identify post-initiation hazards for every covariate stratum reached by q_b, and derive the EIF under that condition.

After the critical law is made well-posed, sharpen OEQ 2 to a named honest-diameter order or a stated dichotomy between nb_n→∞ and nb_n→lambda, with an explicit candidate non-Gaussian confidence construction.

recommended_next_step: Revise the intervention, overlap, and self-normalized critical-statistic definitions; then resubmit this otherwise field-level novel phase-transition angle for Stage -0.5 review.
